monk.classFeatures.9.name: Дополнительная атака
monk.description: Мастер боевых искусств, использующий внутреннюю энергию ки для невероятных подвигов.
monk.name: Монах
monk.startingEquipment.optionA.2: Artisan's Tools or Musical Instrument chosen for the tool proficiency above
monster-hunter.classFeatures.0.descripti: You create or inherit a repository of knowledge known as a Monster Grimoire. Each Monster Hunter carries a grimoire, a manual on dangerous creatures. A Monster Hunter's grimoire is an object entirely unique to each hunter. It may be a leather tome recovered from a dusty library with notes made in the margins, a set of blood-stained scrolls inherited from a previous hunter, or a precious stone with a communing spirit held within.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.0.details.b: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the creature types in your grimoire. For example, if Undead are in your grimoire, you add your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} to an Intelligence ({@skill History}) check to recall lore about an Undead creature you're investigating or a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check to identify claw marks left on a corpse by an Undead creature. If you are already proficient in a skill relating to the monster types in your grimoire, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} instead.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.0.details.c: Choose two types of creatures that you are specialized in hunting from the following list: Aberrations, Beasts, Celestials, Constructs, Dragons, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, Giants, Monstrosities, Oozes, Plants, or Undead.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.0.details.l: When you gain this feature, you also learn one language based on the chart below.

If a Monster Hunter ever loses a grimoire, the hunter has retained enough knowledge to not suffer immediate drawbacks. However, the hunter can't add to the grimoire until it is restored and therefore can't take another Monster Hunter level until a lost grimoire has been replaced. A grimoire can be recreated by spending 8 hours and 50 GP worth of materials such as books, scrolls, and ink.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.0.name: Monster Grimoire
monster-hunter.classFeatures.1.descripti: Your training with weapons allows you to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Longbows and Longswords. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices. When you reach certain Monster Hunter levels, you gain the ability to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of more kinds of weapons, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Monster Hunter Features table.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.1.name: Мастерство оружия
monster-hunter.classFeatures.10.descript: You gain a feature from your Monster Hunter subclass.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.10.name: Subclass Feature
monster-hunter.classFeatures.11.descript: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.11.name: Дополнительная атака
monster-hunter.classFeatures.12.descript: Your Monster Grimoire is further expanded by your encounters and studies. You can select a fourth creature type to add to your Monster Grimoire from the following list: Aberrations, Beasts, Celestials, Constructs, Dragons, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, Giants, Monstrosities, Oozes, Plants, or Undead. You can't choose a monster type you have chosen before. All the benefits you gain from your Monster Grimoire now also apply to this new monster type.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.12.name: Improved Monster Grimoire
monster-hunter.classFeatures.13.descript: You are experienced at navigating the threats that arise near a monster's lair. You have {@status Advantage} on saving throws and {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the damage of lair actions and regional effects caused by creatures. In addition, you have {@status Advantage} on saving throws and {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to damage against the Legendary Actions of creature types in your Monster Grimoire.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.13.name: Lair Sense
monster-hunter.classFeatures.14.descript: You gain a feature from your Monster Hunter subclass.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.14.name: Subclass Feature
monster-hunter.classFeatures.15.descript: You're able to coordinate with your allies to quickly slay dangerous monsters. When you use Studied Response, choose a Friendly creature that can see or hear you. That creature can use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against the creature that triggered your Studied Response. If the ally does not have their {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, they regain it but then must use it to make the attack. Your ally's attack occurs after yours but before the target creature rolls its own attack. This ability refreshes at the start of your turn.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.15.name: Slayer's Aid
monster-hunter.classFeatures.16.descript: You gain a feature from your Monster Hunter subclass.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.16.name: Subclass Feature
monster-hunter.classFeatures.17.descript: You gain an {@variantrule Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.17.name: Эпическое благо
monster-hunter.classFeatures.18.descript: You gain the ability to strike mortal blows against the creatures you have studied. Your attack rolls with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} against creature types in your Monster Grimoire can now score a Critical Hit on a roll of 18 through 20 on the {@dice d20}. When you score a Critical Hit against a creature type in your Monster Grimoire, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw. The DC equals the damage taken, up to a maximum DC of 30. On a failed save, the creature drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. On a successful save, the creature takes the attack's normal damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.18.name: Grave Strike
monster-hunter.classFeatures.2.descripti: You gain a {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat of your choice.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.2.name: Боевой стиль
monster-hunter.classFeatures.3.descripti: You have learned the most effective way to slay a monster is to strike its most vulnerable area at the right moment. Too early, and your attack glances away. Too late, and the monster may eviscerate you. When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you targets you or another creature with a melee or ranged attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} before the attack roll to make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against that creature. If the attack misses, you regain the use of your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.3.name: Studied Response
monster-hunter.classFeatures.4.descripti: You gain a Monster Hunter subclass of your choice. The Carver Guild, Devourer Guild, Occultist Guild, and Trapper Guild subclasses are detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Monster Hunter levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Monster Hunter level or lower.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.4.name: Monster Hunter Subclass
monster-hunter.classFeatures.5.descripti: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monster Hunter levels 8, 12, and 16.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.5.name: Увеличение Характеристик
monster-hunter.classFeatures.6.descripti: You learn the weaknesses of monsters so that you know exactly where to strike to inflict the most damage. You can add your Intelligence modifier to the attack and damage rolls of your weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB}.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.6.name: Expert Strike
monster-hunter.classFeatures.7.descripti: You have expanded your Monster Grimoire with your experience and knowledge. You can select a third creature type to add to your Monster Grimoire from the following list: Aberrations, Beasts, Celestials, Constructs, Dragons, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, Giants, Monstrosities, Oozes, Plants, or Undead. You can't choose a creature type you have chosen before. All the benefits you gain from your Monster Grimoire now also apply to this new type. Additionally, your attack rolls with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} against creature types in your Monster Grimoire can score a Critical Hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.7.name: Improved Monster Grimoire
monster-hunter.classFeatures.8.descripti: You gain a feature from your Monster Hunter subclass.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.8.name: Subclass Feature
monster-hunter.classFeatures.9.descripti: You can rely on your knowledge to anticipate a monster's actions and summon your best defense. Whenever a creature type in your Monster Grimoire forces you to make a saving throw, you can make an Intelligence saving throw in place of that saving throw instead.
monster-hunter.classFeatures.9.name: Knowledgeable Defense
monster-hunter.description: Закалённый охотник, посвятивший жизнь уничтожению сверхъестественных угроз. Использует знания о монстрах и древние техники для выслеживания и уничтожения порождений тьмы.
monster-hunter.name: Охотник на монстров
paladin.classFeatures.0.description: Your blessed touch can heal wounds. You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you take a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. With that pool, you can restore a total number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to five times your Paladin level.
paladin.classFeatures.0.details.healing: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can touch a creature (which could be yourself) and draw power from the pool of healing to restore a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to that creature, up to the maximum amount remaining in the pool.
paladin.classFeatures.0.details.poisonRe: In addition, you can expend 5 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} from the pool of healing power to remove the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition from the creature; those points don't also restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to the creature.
paladin.classFeatures.0.name: Lay on Hands
paladin.classFeatures.1.description: You have learned to cast spells through prayer and meditation. See {@variantrule chapter 7|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Paladin spells, which appear on the {@filter Paladin spell list} later in the class's description.
paladin.classFeatures.1.details.changing: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with other Paladin spells for which you have spell slots.
paladin.classFeatures.1.details.prepared: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 spells from the {@filter Paladin spell list}. {@spell Command|XPHB} and {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Paladin levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Paladin Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the {@filter Paladin spell list} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. If another Paladin feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Paladin spells for you.
paladin.classFeatures.1.details.spellSlo: The Paladin Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
paladin.classFeatures.1.details.spellcas: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Paladin spells.
You can use a {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Paladin spells.
paladin.classFeatures.1.name: Использование заклинаний
paladin.classFeatures.10.description: You radiate a protective, invisible aura in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} that originates from you. The aura is inactive while you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. You and your allies in the aura gain a bonus to saving throws equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum bonus of +1). If another Paladin grants you this bonus, you use only the highest bonus.
paladin.classFeatures.10.name: Aura of Protection
paladin.classFeatures.11.description: You gain a feature from your Paladin subclass.
paladin.classFeatures.11.name: Subclass Feature
paladin.classFeatures.12.description: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of this class's Channel Divinity to overwhelm foes with divine awe. As you present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} or weapon, you can target a number of creatures you can see within 60 feet of yourself equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. On a failed save, the target also has the {@condition Dazed|XPHB} condition for the same duration. The {@condition Frightened|XPHB} target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a successful save.
paladin.classFeatures.12.name: Abjure Foes
paladin.classFeatures.13.description: You and your allies in your Aura of Protection can't have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition while you are conscious.
paladin.classFeatures.13.name: Aura of Courage
paladin.classFeatures.14.description: Your strikes carry divine power. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d8} Radiant damage.
paladin.classFeatures.14.name: Radiant Strikes
paladin.classFeatures.15.description: When you use Lay on Hands on a creature, you can also remove one or more of the following conditions from the creature: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}. You must expend 5 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} from the healing pool for each of these conditions you remove; those points don't also restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to the creature.
paladin.classFeatures.15.name: Restoring Touch
paladin.classFeatures.16.description: You gain a feature from your Paladin subclass.
paladin.classFeatures.16.name: Subclass Feature
paladin.classFeatures.17.description: Your Aura of Protection is now a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB}.
paladin.classFeatures.17.name: Aura Expansion
paladin.classFeatures.18.description: You gain an {@variantrule Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Truesight|XPHB} is recommended.
paladin.classFeatures.18.name: Эпическое благо
paladin.classFeatures.19.description: You gain a feature from your Paladin subclass.
paladin.classFeatures.19.name: Subclass Feature
paladin.classFeatures.2.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices. When you reach certain Paladin levels, you gain the ability to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of more kinds of weapons, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Paladin Features table.
paladin.classFeatures.2.name: Мастерство оружия
paladin.classFeatures.3.description: You gain a {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat of your choice. {@feat Defense|XPHB} is recommended. Whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can replace the feat you chose with a different {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat.
paladin.classFeatures.3.name: Боевой стиль
paladin.classFeatures.4.description: You always have the {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB} spell prepared. In addition, you can cast it without expending a spell slot, but you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again.
paladin.classFeatures.4.name: Paladin's Smite
paladin.classFeatures.5.description: You can channel divine energy directly from the Outer Planes, using that energy to fuel the magical effects described below. You can use this class's Channel Divinity twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You gain additional uses when you reach certain Paladin levels, as shown in the Channel Divinity column of the Paladin Features table. If a Channel Divinity effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals the spell save DC from this class's Spellcasting feature.
paladin.classFeatures.5.details.divineSe: {@b Divine Sense.} As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can open your awareness to detect Celestials, Fiends, and Undead. For the next 10 minutes or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, you know the location of any creature of those types within 60 feet of yourself, and you know its creature type. Within the same radius, you also detect the presence of any place or object that has been consecrated or desecrated, as with the {@spell Hallow|XPHB} spell.
paladin.classFeatures.5.name: Божественный канал
paladin.classFeatures.6.description: You gain a Paladin subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Paladin levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Paladin level or lower.
paladin.classFeatures.6.name: Paladin Subclass
paladin.classFeatures.7.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Paladin levels 8, 12, and 16.
paladin.classFeatures.7.name: Увеличение Характеристик
paladin.classFeatures.8.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.
paladin.classFeatures.8.name: Дополнительная атака
paladin.classFeatures.9.description: You can cast the {@spell Find Steed|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. You can also cast the spell as normal using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. When you cast this spell, the steed is Celestial, and you always have the spell prepared.
paladin.classFeatures.9.name: Faithful Steed
paladin.description: Святой воин, связанный священной клятвой. Сочетает божественную магию с боевым мастерством.
paladin.name: Паладин
ranger.classFeatures.0.description: You have learned to channel the magical essence of nature to cast spells. See {@variantrule chapter 7|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Ranger spells, which appear on the {@filter Ranger spell list} later in the class's description.
ranger.classFeatures.0.details.changingP: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with other Ranger spells for which you have spell slots.
ranger.classFeatures.0.details.preparedS: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Ranger spells. {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} and {@spell Ensnaring Strike|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Ranger levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Ranger Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Ranger spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. If another Ranger feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Ranger spells for you.
ranger.classFeatures.0.details.spellSlot: The Ranger Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
ranger.classFeatures.0.details.spellcast: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your Ranger spells.
You can use a {@item Druidic Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Ranger spells.
ranger.classFeatures.0.name: Использование заклинаний
ranger.classFeatures.1.description: You always have the {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it twice without expending a spell slot, and you regain all expended uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The number of times you can cast the spell without a spell slot increases when you reach certain Ranger levels, as shown in the Favored Enemy column of the Ranger Features table.
ranger.classFeatures.1.name: Избранный враг
ranger.classFeatures.10.description: Choose two of your skill proficiencies with which you lack {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in those skills.
ranger.classFeatures.10.name: Мастерство (Expertise)
ranger.classFeatures.11.description: Primal forces now help fuel you on your journeys, granting you the following benefits.
ranger.classFeatures.11.details.decrease: {@b Decrease Exhaustion.} Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, your {@variantrule Exhaustion|XPHB} level, if any, decreases by 1.
ranger.classFeatures.11.details.temporar: {@b Temporary Hit Points.} As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can give yourself a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@damage 1d8} plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1). You can use this action a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
ranger.classFeatures.11.name: Tireless
ranger.classFeatures.12.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger subclass.
ranger.classFeatures.12.name: Subclass Feature
ranger.classFeatures.13.description: Taking damage can't break your {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} on {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}.
ranger.classFeatures.13.name: Relentless Hunter
ranger.classFeatures.14.description: You invoke spirits of nature to magically hide yourself. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can give yourself the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
ranger.classFeatures.14.name: Nature's Veil
ranger.classFeatures.15.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger subclass.
ranger.classFeatures.15.name: Subclass Feature
ranger.classFeatures.16.description: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the creature currently marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}.
ranger.classFeatures.16.name: Precise Hunter
ranger.classFeatures.17.description: Your connection to the forces of nature grants you {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet.
ranger.classFeatures.17.name: Feral Senses
ranger.classFeatures.18.description: You gain an {@variantrule Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Dimensional Travel|XPHB} is recommended.
ranger.classFeatures.18.name: Эпическое благо
ranger.classFeatures.19.description: The damage die of your {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} is a d10 rather than a d6.
ranger.classFeatures.19.name: Foe Slayer
ranger.classFeatures.2.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.
ranger.classFeatures.2.name: Мастерство оружия
ranger.classFeatures.3.description: Thanks to your extensive wandering, you gain the following benefits.
ranger.classFeatures.3.details.expertise: {@b Expertise.} Choose two of your skill proficiencies with which you lack {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in those skills.
ranger.classFeatures.3.details.languages: {@b Languages.} You learn two languages of your choice.
ranger.classFeatures.3.name: Deft Explorer
ranger.classFeatures.4.description: You gain a {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat of your choice. {@feat Archery|XPHB} is recommended. Whenever you gain a Ranger level, you can replace the feat you chose with a different {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat.
ranger.classFeatures.4.name: Боевой стиль
ranger.classFeatures.5.description: You gain a Ranger subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Ranger levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Ranger level or lower.
ranger.classFeatures.5.name: Ranger Subclass
ranger.classFeatures.6.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Ranger levels 8, 12, and 16.
ranger.classFeatures.6.name: Увеличение Характеристик
ranger.classFeatures.7.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.
ranger.classFeatures.7.name: Дополнительная атака
ranger.classFeatures.8.description: Your Speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor. You also have a Climb Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.
ranger.classFeatures.8.name: Roving
ranger.classFeatures.9.description: You gain a feature from your Ranger subclass.
ranger.classFeatures.9.name: Subclass Feature
ranger.description: Воин дикой природы, мастер выживания и следопытства. Непревзойдённый охотник.
ranger.name: Следопыт
rogue.classFeatures.0.description:
You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. {@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB} and {@skill Stealth|XPHB} are recommended if you have proficiency in them.

At Rogue level 6, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.
rogue.classFeatures.0.name: Мастерство (Expertise)
rogue.classFeatures.1.description:
You know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe's distraction. Once per turn, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage to one creature you hit with an attack roll if you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll and the attack uses a Finesse or a Ranged weapon. The extra damage's type is the same as the weapon's type.

You don't need {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the target, the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition, and you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.

The extra damage increases as you gain Rogue levels, as shown in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue Features table.
rogue.classFeatures.1.name: Скрытая атака
rogue.classFeatures.10.description: You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in two more of your Skill Proficiencies of your choice.
rogue.classFeatures.10.name: Мастерство (Expertise)
rogue.classFeatures.11.description: You can nimbly dodge out of the way of certain dangers. When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. You can't use this feature if you have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition.
rogue.classFeatures.11.name: Evasion
rogue.classFeatures.12.description: Whenever you make an ability check that uses one of your skill or tool proficiencies, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10.
rogue.classFeatures.12.name: Reliable Talent
rogue.classFeatures.13.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
rogue.classFeatures.13.name: Увеличение Характеристик
rogue.classFeatures.14.description: You gain a feature from your Rogue Subclass.
rogue.classFeatures.14.name: Subclass Feature
rogue.classFeatures.15.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
rogue.classFeatures.15.name: Увеличение Характеристик
rogue.classFeatures.16.description: You can use up to two Cunning Strike effects when you deal Sneak Attack damage, paying the die cost for each effect.
rogue.classFeatures.16.name: Improved Cunning Strike
rogue.classFeatures.17.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
rogue.classFeatures.17.name: Увеличение Характеристик
rogue.classFeatures.18.description: You gain a feature from your Rogue Subclass.
rogue.classFeatures.18.name: Subclass Feature
rogue.classFeatures.19.description: When you attack, you know how to exploit a target's distraction. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any attack roll that targets a creature that is within 5 feet of at least one of your allies who doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition.
rogue.classFeatures.19.name: Subtle Strikes
rogue.classFeatures.2.description: You picked up various languages in the communities where you plied your roguish talents. You know {@language Thieves' Cant|XPHB} and one other language of your choice, which you choose from the language tables in chapter 2.
rogue.classFeatures.2.name: Thieves' Cant
rogue.classFeatures.20.description:
You've practiced new ways to use your Sneak Attack deviously. The following effects are now among your Cunning Strike options.

{@b Daze (Cost: 2d6).} The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or on its next turn, it can do only one of the following: move or take an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

{@b Knock Out (Cost: 6d6).} The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or it has the {@condition Unconscious} condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. The {@condition Unconscious} target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

{@b Obscure (Cost: 3d6).} The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw, or it has the {@condition Blinded} condition until the end of its next turn.
rogue.classFeatures.20.name: Devious Strikes
rogue.classFeatures.21.description: Your cunning mind is exceptionally difficult to control. You gain proficiency in Wisdom and Charisma saving throws.
rogue.classFeatures.21.name: Slippery Mind
rogue.classFeatures.22.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
rogue.classFeatures.22.name: Увеличение Характеристик
rogue.classFeatures.23.description: You gain a feature from your Rogue Subclass.
rogue.classFeatures.23.name: Subclass Feature
rogue.classFeatures.24.description: You're so evasive that attackers rarely gain the upper hand against you. No attack roll can have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} against you unless you have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition.
rogue.classFeatures.24.name: Elusive
rogue.classFeatures.25.description: You gain an {@variantrule Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of the Night Spirit|XPHB} is recommended.
rogue.classFeatures.25.name: Эпическое благо
rogue.classFeatures.26.description:
You have a marvelous knack for succeeding when you need to. If you fail a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can turn the roll into a 20.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
rogue.classFeatures.26.name: Stroke of Luck
rogue.classFeatures.3.description:
Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as {@item Daggers|XPHB} and {@item Shortbows|XPHB}.

Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of {@item Scimitars|XPHB} and {@item Shortswords|XPHB}.
rogue.classFeatures.3.name: Мастерство оружия
rogue.classFeatures.4.description: Your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}: {@action Dash}, {@action Disengage}, or {@action Hide}.
rogue.classFeatures.4.name: Cunning Action
rogue.classFeatures.5.description: You gain a Rogue subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Rogue levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Rogue level or lower.
rogue.classFeatures.5.name: Rogue Subclass
rogue.classFeatures.6.description: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you give yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this feature only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use it, your Speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.
rogue.classFeatures.6.name: Steady Aim
rogue.classFeatures.7.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Rogue levels 8, 10, 12, and 16.
rogue.classFeatures.7.name: Увеличение Характеристик
rogue.classFeatures.8.description:
You've developed cunning ways to use your Sneak Attack. When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can add one of the following Cunning Strike effects. Each effect has a die cost, which is the number of Sneak Attack damage dice you must forgo to add the effect. You remove the die before rolling, and the effect occurs immediately after the attack's damage is dealt. For example, if you add the Poison effect, remove {@damage 1d6} from the Sneak Attack's damage before rolling.

If a Cunning Strike effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

{@b Poison (Cost: 1d6).} You add a toxin to your strike, forcing the target to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Poisoned} condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the Poisoned target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.
To use this effect, you must have a {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB} on your person.

{@b Trip (Cost: 1d6).} If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition Prone} condition.

{@b Withdraw (Cost: 1d6).} Immediately after the attack, you move up to half your Speed without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.
rogue.classFeatures.8.name: Cunning Strike
rogue.classFeatures.9.description: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to halve the attack's damage against you (round down).
rogue.classFeatures.9.name: Uncanny Dodge
rogue.description: Ловкий специалист, полагающийся на хитрость и точные удары. Мастер скрытности и обмана.
rogue.name: Плут
sorcerer.classFeatures.0.description: An event in your past left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with simmering magic. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can unleash that magic for 1 minute, during which you gain the following benefits.
sorcerer.classFeatures.0.details.advanta: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack rolls of Sorcerer spells you cast.
sorcerer.classFeatures.0.details.spellSa: The spell save DC of your Sorcerer spells increases by 1.
sorcerer.classFeatures.0.name: Innate Sorcery
sorcerer.classFeatures.1.description: You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sorcerer.classFeatures.1.name: Innate Sorcery: Uses
sorcerer.classFeatures.10.description: If you have no uses of Innate Sorcery left, you can use it if you spend 2 Sorcery Points when you take the {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to activate it. In addition, while your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use up to two of your Metamagic options on each spell you cast.
sorcerer.classFeatures.10.name: Sorcery Incarnate
sorcerer.classFeatures.11.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
sorcerer.classFeatures.11.name: Увеличение Характеристик
sorcerer.classFeatures.12.description: Because your magic flows from within, you can alter your spells to suit your needs; you gain two Metamagic options of your choice from the "Metamagic Options" section later in this class's description. You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless otherwise noted in one of those options. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your Metamagic options with one you don't know.
sorcerer.classFeatures.12.name: Метамагия
sorcerer.classFeatures.13.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
sorcerer.classFeatures.13.name: Увеличение Характеристик

sorcerer.classFeatures.14.name: Subclass Feature

sorcerer.classFeatures.15.name: Увеличение Характеристик
sorcerer.classFeatures.16.description: Because your magic flows from within, you can alter your spells to suit your needs; you gain two Metamagic options of your choice from the "Metamagic Options" section later in this class's description. You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless otherwise noted in one of those options. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your Metamagic options with one you don't know.
sorcerer.classFeatures.16.name: Метамагия
sorcerer.classFeatures.17.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerer subclass.
sorcerer.classFeatures.17.name: Subclass Feature
sorcerer.classFeatures.18.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Dimensional Travel|XPHB} is recommended.
sorcerer.classFeatures.18.name: Эпическое благо
sorcerer.classFeatures.19.description: While your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use one Metamagic option on each of your turns without spending Sorcery Points on it.
sorcerer.classFeatures.19.name: Arcane Apotheosis
sorcerer.classFeatures.2.description: You have learned to cast spells through innate magical talent. See {@variantrule chapter 7|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Sorcerer spells, which appear on the {@filter Sorcerer spell list}.
sorcerer.classFeatures.2.details.cantrip: You know four cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Sorcerer spell list}. {@spell Light|XPHB}, {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB}, {@spell Shocking Grasp|XPHB}, and {@spell Sorcerous Burst|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Sorcerer spell list}. When you reach Sorcerer levels 4 and 10, you learn another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Sorcerer spell list}, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Sorcerer Features table.
sorcerer.classFeatures.2.details.changin: Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Sorcerer spell for which you have spell slots.
sorcerer.classFeatures.2.details.prepare: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 spells from the {@filter Sorcerer spell list}. {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} and {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Sorcerer levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Sorcerer Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the {@filter Sorcerer spell list} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. If another Sorcerer feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Sorcerer spells for you.
sorcerer.classFeatures.2.details.spellSl: The Sorcerer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sorcerer.classFeatures.2.details.spellca: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Sorcerer spells.
You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Sorcerer spells.
sorcerer.classFeatures.2.name: Использование заклинаний
sorcerer.classFeatures.3.description: You can tap into the wellspring of magic within yourself. This wellspring is represented by Sorcery Points, which allow you to create a variety of magical effects.
sorcerer.classFeatures.3.details.convert: {@b Converting a Spell Slot to Sorcery Points.} You can expend a spell slot as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to gain a number of Sorcery Points equal to the slot's level.
sorcerer.classFeatures.3.details.creatin: {@b Creating Spell Slots.} You can transform unexpended Sorcery Points into one spell slot as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. The Creating Spell Slots table shows the cost of creating a spell slot of a given level, and you can't create a slot above level 5. Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sorcerer.classFeatures.3.details.sorcery: You have a number of Sorcery Points equal to your Sorcerer level, as shown in the Sorcery Points column of the Sorcerer Features table. You can spend Sorcery Points on various abilities. You regain all expended Sorcery Points when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sorcerer.classFeatures.3.name: Font of Magic
sorcerer.classFeatures.4.description: The cost of creating a spell slot: Level 1 = 2 Sorcery Points, Level 2 = 3 Sorcery Points, Level 3 = 5 Sorcery Points, Level 4 = 6 Sorcery Points, Level 5 = 7 Sorcery Points.
sorcerer.classFeatures.4.name: Creating Spell Slots
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.description: Because your magic flows from within, you can alter your spells to suit your needs; you gain two Metamagic options of your choice from the "Metamagic Options" section later in this class's description. You gain two more at Sorcerer levels 10 and 17. If a Metamagic option requires a saving throw, the DC equals your spell save DC. You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless otherwise noted.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.careful: {@b Careful Spell.} When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, you can protect some of those creatures from the spell's full force. To do so, spend 1 Sorcery Point and choose a number of those creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell, and it takes no damage if it would normally take half damage on a successful save.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.distant: {@b Distant Spell.} When you cast a spell that has a range of at least 5 feet, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to double the range of the spell. Or when you cast a spell that has a range of Touch, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to make the range of the spell 30 feet.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.empower: {@b Empowered Spell.} When you roll damage for a spell, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one), and you must use the new rolls. You can use Empowered Spell even if you've already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.extende: {@b Extended Spell.} When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to double its duration to a maximum duration of 24 hours. If the affected spell requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any saving throw you make to maintain that {@status Concentration|XPHB}.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.heighte: {@b Heightened Spell.} When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to give one target of the spell {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saves against the spell.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.quicken: {@b Quickened Spell.} When you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to change the casting time to a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} for this casting. You can't modify a spell in this way if you've already cast a level 1+ spell on the current turn, nor can you cast a level 1+ spell on this turn after modifying a spell in this way.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.seeking: {@b Seeking Spell.} If you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to reroll the {@dice d20}, and you must use the new roll. You can use Seeking Spell even if you've already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.subtleS: {@b Subtle Spell.} When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to cast it without any Verbal, Somatic, or Material components, except Material components that are consumed by the spell or that have a cost specified in the spell.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.transmu: {@b Transmuted Spell.} When you cast a spell that deals a type of damage from the following list, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to change that damage type to one of the other listed types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Thunder.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.details.twinned: {@b Twinned Spell.} When you cast a spell, such as {@spell Charm Person|XPHB}, that can be cast with a higher-level spell slot to target an additional creature, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to increase the spell's effective level by 1.
sorcerer.classFeatures.5.name: Метамагия
sorcerer.classFeatures.6.description: You gain a Sorcerer subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Sorcerer levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Sorcerer level or lower.
sorcerer.classFeatures.6.name: Sorcerer Subclass
sorcerer.classFeatures.7.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Sorcerer levels 8, 12, and 16.
sorcerer.classFeatures.7.name: Увеличение Характеристик
sorcerer.classFeatures.8.description: When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can regain expended Sorcery Points, but no more than a number equal to half your Sorcerer level (round down). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
sorcerer.classFeatures.8.name: Sorcery Restoration
sorcerer.classFeatures.9.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerer subclass.
sorcerer.classFeatures.9.name: Subclass Feature
sorcerer.description: Врождённый маг, чья сила проистекает из крови или судьбы. Управляет сырой магической энергией.
sorcerer.name: Чародей
warlock.classFeatures.0.description: You have unearthed Eldritch Invocations, pieces of forbidden knowledge that imbue you with an abiding magical ability or other lessons. You gain one invocation of your choice, such as {@invocation Pact of the Tome|XPHB}. Invocations are described in the "Eldritch Invocation Options" section later in this class's description.
warlock.classFeatures.0.details.prerequi: If an invocation has a prerequisite, you must meet it to learn that invocation. For example, if an invocation requires you to be a level 5+ Warlock, you can select the invocation once you reach Warlock level 5.
warlock.classFeatures.0.details.replacin:
Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your invocations with another one for which you qualify. You can't replace an invocation if it's a prerequisite for another invocation that you have.

When you gain certain Warlock levels, you gain more invocations of your choice, as shown in the Invocations column of the Warlock Features table.

You can't pick the same invocation more than once unless its description says otherwise.
warlock.classFeatures.0.name: Eldritch Invocations
warlock.classFeatures.1.description: Through occult ceremony, you have formed a pact with a mysterious entity to gain magical powers. The entity is a voice in the shadows—its identity unclear—but its boon to you is concrete: the ability to cast spells. See {@variantrule chapter 7|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Warlock spells, which appear in the {@filter Warlock spell list} later in the class's description.
warlock.classFeatures.1.details.cantrips:
You know two Warlock cantrips of your choice. {@spell Eldritch Blast|XPHB} and {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Warlock cantrip of your choice.

When you reach Warlock levels 4 and 10, you learn another Warlock cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Warlock Features table.
warlock.classFeatures.1.details.changing: Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Warlock spell of an eligible level.
warlock.classFeatures.1.details.prepared:
You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Warlock spells. {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} and {@spell Hex|XPHB} are recommended.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Warlock levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Warlock Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Warlock spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the table. The chosen spells must be of a level no higher than what's shown in the table's Slot Level column for your level. When you reach level 6, for example, you learn a new Warlock spell, which can be of levels 1–3.

If another Warlock feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Warlock spells for you.
warlock.classFeatures.1.details.spellSlo:
The Warlock Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your Warlock spells of levels 1–5. The table also shows the level of those slots, all of which are the same level. You regain all expended Pact Magic spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

For example, when you're a level 5 Warlock, you have two level 3 spell slots. To cast the level 1 spell {@spell Witch Bolt|XPHB}, you must spend one of those slots, and you cast it as a level 3 spell.
warlock.classFeatures.1.details.spellcas: Charisma is the spellcasting ability for your Warlock spells.
You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Warlock spells.
warlock.classFeatures.1.name: Pact Magic
warlock.classFeatures.10.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
warlock.classFeatures.10.name: Увеличение Характеристик
warlock.classFeatures.11.description: You gain a level 7 Warlock spell of your choice.
warlock.classFeatures.11.name: Mystic Arcanum (Level 7 Spell)
warlock.classFeatures.12.description: You gain a feature from your Warlock subclass.
warlock.classFeatures.12.name: Subclass Feature
warlock.classFeatures.13.description: You gain a level 8 Warlock spell of your choice.
warlock.classFeatures.13.name: Mystic Arcanum (Level 8 Spell)
warlock.classFeatures.14.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
warlock.classFeatures.14.name: Увеличение Характеристик
warlock.classFeatures.15.description: You gain a level 9 Warlock spell of your choice.
warlock.classFeatures.15.name: Mystic Arcanum (Level 9 Spell)
warlock.classFeatures.16.description: You gain an {@feat Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Fate|XPHB} is recommended.
warlock.classFeatures.16.name: Эпическое благо
warlock.classFeatures.17.description: When you use your Magical Cunning feature, you regain all your expended Pact Magic spell slots.
warlock.classFeatures.17.name: Eldritch Master
warlock.classFeatures.2.description: You can perform an esoteric rite for 1 minute. At the end of it, you regain expended Pact Magic spell slots but no more than a number equal to half your maximum (round up). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
warlock.classFeatures.2.name: Magical Cunning
warlock.classFeatures.3.description: You gain a Warlock subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Warlock levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Warlock level or lower.
warlock.classFeatures.3.name: Warlock Subclass
warlock.classFeatures.4.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Warlock levels 8, 12, and 16.
warlock.classFeatures.4.name: Увеличение Характеристик
warlock.classFeatures.5.description: You gain a feature from your Warlock subclass.
warlock.classFeatures.5.name: Subclass Feature
warlock.classFeatures.6.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
warlock.classFeatures.6.name: Увеличение Характеристик
warlock.classFeatures.7.description:
In the past, you usually contacted your patron through intermediaries. Now you can communicate directly; you always have the {@spell Contact Other Plane|XPHB} spell prepared. With this feature, you can cast the spell without expending a spell slot to contact your patron, and you automatically succeed on the spell's saving throw.

Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
warlock.classFeatures.7.name: Contact Patron
warlock.classFeatures.8.description: You gain a feature from your Warlock subclass.
warlock.classFeatures.8.name: Subclass Feature
warlock.classFeatures.9.description:
Your patron grants you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one level 6 spell from the Warlock spell list as this arcanum. You can cast your arcanum spell once without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again.

As shown in the Warlock Features table, you gain another Warlock arcanum of your choice when you reach Warlock levels 13 (level 7 spell), 15 (level 8 spell), and 17 (level 9 spell). You regain all uses of your Mystic Arcanum when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
warlock.classFeatures.9.details.replacin: Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one of your arcanum spells with another Warlock spell of the same level.
warlock.classFeatures.9.name: Mystic Arcanum (Level 6 Spell)
warlock.description: Маг, заключивший пакт с могущественной сущностью. Получает уникальные силы от своего покровителя.
warlock.name: Колдун
wizard.classFeatures.0.description: As a student of arcane magic, you have learned to cast spells. See {@variantrule chapter 7|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Wizard spells, which appear on the {@filter Wizard spell list}.
wizard.classFeatures.0.details.cantrips: You know three Wizard cantrips of your choice. {@spell Light|XPHB}, {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB}, and {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Wizard cantrip of your choice. When you reach Wizard levels 4 and 10, you learn another Wizard cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Wizard Features table.
wizard.classFeatures.0.details.changingP: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with spells from your spellbook.
wizard.classFeatures.0.details.copyingSp: {@b Copying a Spell into the Book.} When you find a level 1+ Wizard spell, you can copy it into your spellbook if it's of a level you can prepare and if you have time to copy it. For each level of the spell, the transcription takes 2 hours and costs 50 GP. Afterward you can prepare the spell like the other spells in your spellbook.
wizard.classFeatures.0.details.copyingTh: {@b Copying the Book.} You can copy a spell from your spellbook into another book. This is like copying a new spell into your spellbook but faster, since you already know how to cast the spell. You need spend only 1 hour and 10 GP for each level of the copied spell. If you lose your spellbook, you can use the same procedure to transcribe the Wizard spells that you have prepared into a new spellbook. Filling out the remainder of the new book requires you to find new spells to do so. For this reason, many wizards keep a backup spellbook.
wizard.classFeatures.0.details.expanding: The spells you add to your spellbook as you gain levels reflect your ongoing magical research, but you might find other spells during your adventures that you can add to the book. You could discover a Wizard spell on a {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB}, for example, and then copy it into your spellbook.
wizard.classFeatures.0.details.preparedS:
You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To do so, choose four spells from your spellbook. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Wizard levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Wizard Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Wizard spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 3 Wizard, your list of prepared spells can include six spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination, chosen from your spellbook.

If another Wizard feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Wizard spells for you.
wizard.classFeatures.0.details.spellSlot: The Wizard Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
wizard.classFeatures.0.details.spellbook:
Your wizardly apprenticeship culminated in the creation of a unique book: your spellbook. It is a Tiny object that weighs 3 pounds, contains 100 pages, and can be read only by you or someone casting {@spell Identify|XPHB}. You determine the book's appearance and materials, such as a gilt-edged tome or a collection of vellum bound with twine.

The book contains the level 1+ spells you know. It starts with six level 1 Wizard spells of your choice. {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Feather Fall|XPHB}, {@spell Mage Armor|XPHB}, {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB}, {@spell Sleep|XPHB}, and {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB} are recommended.

Whenever you gain a Wizard level after 1, add two Wizard spells of your choice to your spellbook. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots, as shown in the Wizard Features table. The spells are the culmination of arcane research you do regularly.
wizard.classFeatures.0.details.spellcast: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Wizard spells.
You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} or your spellbook as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Wizard spells.
wizard.classFeatures.0.name: Использование заклинаний
wizard.classFeatures.1.description: You can cast any spell as a Ritual if that spell has the Ritual tag and the spell is in your spellbook. You needn't have the spell prepared, but you must read from the book to cast a spell in this way.
wizard.classFeatures.1.name: Ritual Adept
wizard.classFeatures.10.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
wizard.classFeatures.10.name: Увеличение Характеристик
wizard.classFeatures.11.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard Subclass.
wizard.classFeatures.11.name: Subclass Feature
wizard.classFeatures.12.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
wizard.classFeatures.12.name: Увеличение Характеристик
wizard.classFeatures.13.description:
You have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose a level 1 and a level 2 spell in your spellbook that have a casting time of an action. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.

Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can study your spellbook and replace one of those spells with an eligible spell of the same level from the book.
wizard.classFeatures.13.name: Spell Mastery
wizard.classFeatures.14.description: You gain an {@feat Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Spell Recall|XPHB} is recommended.
wizard.classFeatures.14.name: Эпическое благо
wizard.classFeatures.15.description: Choose two level 3 spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each of them once at level 3 without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you can't cast them in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. To cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot.
wizard.classFeatures.15.name: Signature Spells
wizard.classFeatures.2.description:
You can regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than half your Wizard level (round up), and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher. For example, if you're a level 4 Wizard, you can recover up to two levels' worth of spell slots, regaining either one level 2 spell slot or two level 1 spell slots.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
wizard.classFeatures.2.name: Arcane Recovery
wizard.classFeatures.3.description: While studying magic, you also specialized in another field of study. Choose one of the following skills in which you have proficiency: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion. You have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in the chosen skill.
wizard.classFeatures.3.name: Scholar
wizard.classFeatures.4.description: You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. The Abjurer, Diviner, Evoker, and Illusionist subclasses are detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Wizard levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are unlocked or enhanced at the Wizard levels specified in the subclass.
wizard.classFeatures.4.name: Wizard Subclass
wizard.classFeatures.5.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Wizard levels 8, 12, and 16.
wizard.classFeatures.5.name: Увеличение Характеристик
wizard.classFeatures.6.description: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1+ Wizard spells you have prepared for your Spellcasting feature with another level 1+ spell from the book.
wizard.classFeatures.6.name: Memorize Spell
wizard.classFeatures.7.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard Subclass.
wizard.classFeatures.7.name: Subclass Feature
wizard.classFeatures.8.description: Вы получаете черту {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB|Увеличение характеристик} или другую черту на ваш выбор, требованиям которой вы соответствуете.
wizard.classFeatures.8.name: Увеличение Характеристик
wizard.classFeatures.9.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard Subclass.
wizard.classFeatures.9.name: Subclass Feature
wizard.description: Учёный маг, постигающий тайны мироздания через изучение заклинаний. Самый универсальный заклинатель.
wizard.name: Волшебник
